functions.name: handle_hire
functions.docstring:
Handle POST /employees — create a new hire.

Validates the request body, delegates to EmployeeService.hire_employee,
and returns the created employee record with a 201 status.
functions.summary: Handles the onboarding of a new employee by validating required hiring data and delegating to the employee service to execute the hire workflow, returning the created employee record.

functions.name: handle_get_employee
functions.docstring: Handle GET /employees/{id} — retrieve employee details.
functions.summary: Retrieves and returns the details of a specific employee by ID, serving as a read endpoint for employee records.

functions.name: handle_promote
functions.docstring:
Handle POST /employees/{id}/promote — promote an employee.

Validates promotion eligibility, salary constraints, and grade band
compliance before executing the promotion.
functions.summary: Handles the promotion of an employee by validating required promotion data and delegating to the employee service, which enforces eligibility, salary constraints, and grade band compliance.

functions.name: handle_transfer
functions.docstring: Handle POST /employees/{id}/transfer — transfer to a new department.
functions.summary: Handles the transfer of an employee to a new department, optionally updating job title and salary, by delegating to the employee service.

functions.name: handle_terminate
functions.docstring:
Handle POST /employees/{id}/terminate — terminate employment.

Triggers the full offboarding workflow: benefit termination, COBRA,
final paycheck, and access revocation.
functions.domain: Human Capital Management / Employee Lifecycle / Offboarding
functions.summary: Handles the termination of an employee's employment, triggering a full offboarding workflow including benefit termination, COBRA processing, final paycheck, and access revocation.

functions.name: handle_org_tree
functions.docstring: Handle GET /employees/{id}/org-tree — get organizational hierarchy.
functions.summary: HTTP API endpoint handler that receives a request for an employee's organisational hierarchy, delegates to the employee service, and returns a formatted HTTP response.

functions.name: _success_response
functions.summary: Constructs a standardised successful HTTP response envelope with the correct status code, JSON content-type header, and serialised body.

functions.name: _error_response
functions.summary: Constructs a standardised error HTTP response envelope with the correct status code, JSON content-type header, and an error message body.

functions.name: handle_create_run
functions.docstring:
Handle POST /payroll/runs — create a new payroll run.

Validates pay period dates and creates a draft run ready for processing.
functions.summary: HTTP API endpoint handler that validates incoming request fields for creating a payroll run, delegates creation to the payroll service, and returns the new run's ID and status.

functions.name: handle_process_run
functions.docstring:
Handle POST /payroll/runs/{id}/process — process payroll.

Calculates gross pay, taxes, deductions, and net pay for all
eligible employees in the pay period.
functions.summary: HTTP API endpoint handler that triggers payroll processing for a given run, delegates the calculation work to the payroll service, and returns a summary of processed employees and financial totals.

functions.name: handle_approve_run
functions.docstring:
Handle POST /payroll/runs/{id}/approve — approve payroll for payment.

Only payroll admins can approve. Triggers ACH payment initiation.
functions.summary: Handles the HTTP endpoint for approving a payroll run for payment, enforcing that an approver is identified and delegating to the payroll service which triggers ACH payment initiation.

functions.name: _success_response
functions.summary: Formats a successful HTTP response with the appropriate status code, JSON content-type header, and serialised body for return to API callers.

functions.name: _error_response
functions.summary: Formats an error HTTP response with the appropriate status code and a JSON error message body for return to API callers.

functions.name: HCMRepository.__init__
functions.summary: Initialises the HCM repository with AWS DynamoDB and S3 clients and configures the table/bucket names used for all data access operations.

functions.name: HCMRepository.get_employee
functions.docstring: Retrieve an employee by ID.
functions.summary: Retrieves a single employee record from DynamoDB by employee ID and deserialises it into a domain object.

functions.name: HCMRepository.save_employee
functions.docstring: Create or update an employee record.
functions.summary: Persists an employee record to the database, handling both creation and updates.

functions.name: HCMRepository.find_active_employees
functions.docstring: Return all non-terminated employees.
functions.summary: Retrieves all employees who have not been terminated from the data store.

functions.name: HCMRepository.find_employees
functions.docstring: Find employees matching the given filters.
functions.summary: Queries and returns a filtered list of employee records based on arbitrary attribute criteria.

functions.name: HCMRepository.count_active_employees
functions.docstring: Count active employees in a department.
functions.summary: Returns the count of active (non-terminated) employees within a specific department.

functions.name: HCMRepository.get_department
functions.docstring: Retrieve a department by ID.
functions.summary: Fetches a single department record from the data store by its identifier.

functions.name: HCMRepository.save_leave_request
functions.docstring: Save a leave request.
functions.summary: Persists a leave request record to the DynamoDB data store.

functions.name: HCMRepository.get_leave_request
functions.docstring: Retrieve a leave request by ID.
functions.summary: Retrieves a single leave request record from the DynamoDB data store by its unique identifier.

functions.name: HCMRepository.find_overlapping_leave
functions.docstring: Check for overlapping approved or pending leave.
functions.summary: Queries the data store to find any existing approved or pending leave records that overlap with a given date range for an employee.

functions.name: HCMRepository.get_leave_balance
functions.docstring: Get leave balance for an employee, type, and year.
functions.summary: Retrieves the leave balance record for a specific employee, leave type, and year from the DynamoDB data store.

functions.name: HCMRepository.save_leave_balance
functions.docstring: Save a leave balance record.
functions.summary: Persists a leave balance record to the DynamoDB data store.

functions.name: HCMRepository.save_payroll_run
functions.docstring: Save a payroll run.
functions.summary: Persists a payroll run record to the DynamoDB payroll table after serializing it.

functions.name: HCMRepository.get_payroll_run
functions.docstring: Retrieve a payroll run by ID.
functions.summary: Retrieves a payroll run record from DynamoDB by its unique run ID and deserializes it into a domain object.

functions.name: HCMRepository.save_pay_stub
functions.docstring: Save a pay stub.
functions.summary: Persists a pay stub record to the DynamoDB payroll table, serializing the stub data as JSON.

functions.name: HCMRepository.get_ytd_gross
functions.docstring: Get year-to-date gross pay for an employee.
functions.summary: Scans the payroll table to calculate the total year-to-date gross pay for a specific employee by aggregating pay stub records.

functions.name: HCMRepository.get_active_enrollments
functions.docstring: Get all active benefit enrollments for an employee.
functions.summary: Queries the benefits table to retrieve all active benefit enrollment records for a given employee.

functions.name: HCMRepository.save_enrollment
functions.docstring: Save a benefit enrollment.
functions.summary: Persists a benefit enrollment record to the DynamoDB benefits table.

functions.name: HCMRepository.get_benefit_plan
functions.docstring: Retrieve a benefit plan by ID.
functions.summary: Retrieves a benefit plan record from the DynamoDB benefits table by plan ID.

functions.name: HCMRepository.save_audit_event
functions.docstring: Persist an audit trail event.
functions.summary: Persists an audit trail event to the DynamoDB audit table for compliance purposes.

functions.name: HCMRepository._deserialize_employee
functions.summary: Deserialises a raw DynamoDB item into an employee domain object.

functions.name: HCMRepository._deserialize_leave
functions.summary: Deserialises a raw DynamoDB item into a leave request domain object.

functions.name: HCMRepository._serialize_balance
functions.summary: Serializes a leave balance object into a DynamoDB-compatible dictionary format with a composite key for storage.

functions.name: HCMRepository._deserialize_balance
functions.summary: Deserializes a raw DynamoDB item back into a leave balance representation by parsing the stored JSON data field.

functions.name: HCMRepository._deserialize_payroll_run
functions.summary: Deserializes a raw DynamoDB item back into a payroll run representation by parsing the stored JSON data field.

functions.name: HCMRepository._deserialize_department
functions.summary: Deserializes a raw DynamoDB item back into a department representation by parsing the stored JSON data field.

functions.name: HCMRepository._deserialize_enrollment
functions.summary: Deserializes a raw DynamoDB item back into a benefits enrollment representation by parsing the stored JSON data field.

functions.name: Employee.tenure_years
functions.docstring: Calculate years of service from hire date.
functions.summary: Calculates an employee's years of service based on hire date and termination date (or today if still active), rounded to one decimal place.

functions.name: Employee.is_eligible_for_benefits
functions.docstring: Employees must be full-time and past 90-day probation to get benefits.
functions.summary: Determines whether an employee qualifies for benefits enrollment by enforcing full-time employment status, non-terminated status, and completion of a 90-day probationary period.

functions.name: Employee.is_eligible_for_promotion
functions.docstring: Must be active, past probation, and have at least 1 year tenure.
functions.summary: Determines whether an employee is eligible for promotion by requiring active employment status and at least one year of tenure.

functions.name: LeaveRequest.business_days
functions.docstring: Calculate the number of business days in the leave period.
functions.summary: Calculates the number of business days (Monday–Friday) within a leave request's date range, excluding weekends.

functions.name: TaxWithholding.total
functions.summary: Computes the total tax withholding amount by summing all individual tax components (federal, state, social security, Medicare, etc.).

functions.name: BenefitsService.enroll_employee
functions.docstring:
Enroll an employee in a benefit plan.

Business rules:
- Employee must be eligible for benefits (full-time, past probation).
- Must be within an enrollment window (new hire, open enrollment, or life event).
- Cannot enroll in the same plan type twice.
- Contribution amounts must not exceed IRS limits.
- Dependent count must not exceed plan maximum.
functions.summary: Orchestrates the end-to-end process of enrolling an employee in a benefit plan, coordinating eligibility checks, enrollment window validation, duplicate detection, contribution calculations, and persistence of the resulting enrollment record.

functions.name: BenefitsService.waive_benefit
functions.docstring:
Waive a benefit type during enrollment window.

Business rules:
- Medical coverage waiver requires proof of alternative coverage.
- Waiver is recorded for compliance tracking.
functions.summary: Enforces the policy that waiving medical coverage requires proof of alternative coverage, and records the waiver for compliance tracking purposes.

functions.name: BenefitsService.terminate_benefits
functions.docstring:
Terminate all active benefit enrollments upon employee separation.

Business rules:
- Benefits continue through the end of the termination month.
- COBRA eligibility is triggered for medical, dental, and vision.
- 401k is rolled over or distributed per employee election.
functions.summary: Manages the full lifecycle process of terminating all active benefit enrollments when an employee separates, including calculating benefit end dates, updating enrollment statuses, and triggering COBRA eligibility notifications.

functions.name: BenefitsService._validate_401k_contribution
functions.docstring:
Validate 401k contribution against IRS limits.

Employees age 50+ get an additional catch-up contribution allowance.
functions.domain: Human Capital Management / Benefits Administration / Regulatory Compliance
functions.summary: Validates that a proposed 401k annual contribution does not exceed IRS statutory limits, applying the age-based catch-up contribution allowance for employees aged 50 or older, and ensures contributions do not exceed the employee's salary.

functions.name: BenefitsService._calculate_401k_match
functions.docstring:
Calculate employer 401k match.

Company matches 50% of employee contributions up to 6% of salary.
functions.domain: Human Capital Management / Benefits Administration / Compensation
functions.summary: Calculates the employer's 401k matching contribution based on the company policy of matching 50% of employee contributions up to 6% of the employee's base salary.

functions.name: BenefitsService._is_in_enrollment_window
functions.docstring: Check if the employee is within a valid enrollment window.
functions.summary: Determines whether an employee is eligible to enroll in benefits by checking three valid enrollment windows: new hire (within 30 days of hire), open enrollment (November), or a qualifying life event.

functions.name: BenefitsService._calculate_effective_date
functions.docstring:
Determine when coverage becomes effective.

New hires: first of the month following 30 days from hire.
Open enrollment: January 1 of the next year.
Life event: first of the month following the event.
functions.summary: Calculates the benefits coverage effective date based on the reason for enrollment: new hire (first of month after 30 days), open enrollment (January 1 next year), or life event (first of next month).

functions.name: BenefitsService._initiate_cobra
functions.docstring:
Initiate COBRA continuation coverage process.

COBRA allows terminated employees to continue group health coverage
for up to 18 months at full cost (employee + employer share) plus
a 2% administrative fee.
functions.summary: Initiates COBRA continuation coverage for a terminated employee by creating new COBRA enrollment records for each eligible plan, calculating the full premium cost plus a 2% administrative fee, and persisting the enrollments.

functions.name: ComplianceService.check_aca_compliance
functions.docstring:
Check ACA compliance for the organization.

Business rules:
- Applicable Large Employers (50+ FTEs) must offer affordable
  minimum essential coverage to full-time employees.
- Full-time = 30+ hours/week or 130+ hours/month.
- Variable-hour employees are measured over a lookback period.
- Non-compliance triggers IRS penalties (Section 4980H).
functions.summary: Executes an ACA compliance check for the organisation by calculating FTE count, determining Applicable Large Employer status, identifying full-time employees lacking medical coverage, and returning a structured compliance report with violation details.

functions.name: ComplianceService._calculate_fte_count
functions.docstring:
Calculate Full-Time Equivalent count for ACA purposes.

Full-time employees count as 1 FTE each.
Part-time hours are aggregated and divided by 120 to get FTE equivalents.
functions.summary: Calculates the Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) headcount for ACA Applicable Large Employer determination by counting full-time employees as 1.0 FTE each and converting aggregated part-time hours to FTE equivalents using the IRS 120-hour divisor.

functions.name: ComplianceService._is_aca_full_time
functions.docstring: Determine if an employee is ACA full-time (30+ hours/week).
functions.summary: Determines whether an employee qualifies as ACA full-time based on employment type or average weekly hours meeting the 30-hour threshold.

functions.name: ComplianceService.check_i9_compliance
functions.docstring:
Audit I-9 form completion for all employees.

Business rules:
- Section 1 must be completed by end of first day of employment.
- Section 2 must be completed within 3 business days of start.
- Reverification required when work authorization expires.
- Missing or late I-9s expose the employer to fines ($252-$2,507 per form).
functions.summary: Audits I-9 form completion across all active employees, identifying missing forms, incomplete sections, and expired or expiring work authorizations, and returns a structured compliance report.

functions.name: ComplianceService.generate_eeo1_report
functions.docstring:
Generate EEO-1 Component 1 workforce demographics report.

Business rules:
- Required for employers with 100+ employees.
- Reports workforce by job category, race/ethnicity, and gender.
- Must be filed annually with the EEOC.
functions.domain: HR Compliance / Equal Employment Opportunity Reporting
functions.summary: Generates the EEO-1 Component 1 workforce demographics report for a given year by aggregating employee data into a job-category-by-demographics matrix and determining filing obligation.

functions.name: ComplianceService._map_to_eeo_category
functions.docstring: Map a job title to an EEO-1 job category.
functions.domain: HR Compliance / Equal Employment Opportunity Reporting
functions.summary: Maps an employee's job title to one of the EEOC-defined EEO-1 job categories using keyword matching against standardised category definitions.

functions.name: ComplianceService.check_overtime_compliance
functions.docstring:
Check FLSA overtime compliance for a pay period.

Business rules:
- Non-exempt employees must be paid 1.5x for hours over 40/week.
- Misclassification of exempt status is a violation.
- State laws may have stricter overtime rules (e.g., CA daily overtime).
functions.summary: Audits FLSA overtime compliance for a pay period by checking all active employees for unpaid overtime and potential exempt-status misclassification, returning a structured violations report.

functions.name: ComplianceService.log_audit_event
functions.docstring:
Record an audit event for compliance tracking.

All sensitive HR actions (hire, terminate, salary change, etc.)
must be logged with who did what, when, and why.
functions.summary: Records a structured audit trail entry for any sensitive HR action, capturing who performed what action, on which entity, when, and from where, to satisfy compliance and regulatory requirements.

functions.name: EmployeeService.hire_employee
functions.docstring:
Create a new employee record and initiate the onboarding process.

Business rules:
- Salary must fall within the position's grade band.
- Department must not exceed its headcount limit.
- New full-time hires start in PROBATION status for 90 days.
- Contract and intern hires start as ACTIVE immediately.
functions.summary: Orchestrates the end-to-end hiring of a new employee: validates department capacity and salary grade band, determines the correct initial employment status based on employment type, persists the new employee record, and triggers the onboarding workflow.

functions.name: EmployeeService.complete_probation
functions.docstring:
Transition an employee from PROBATION to ACTIVE after 90 days.

Business rules:
- Employee must currently be in PROBATION status.
- At least 90 calendar days must have elapsed since hire date.
- Manager approval is assumed (handled upstream).
functions.summary: Enforces the 90-day probation policy by validating that an employee is currently in PROBATION status and that at least 90 calendar days have elapsed since hire before transitioning them to ACTIVE status.

functions.name: EmployeeService.transfer_employee
functions.docstring:
Transfer an employee to a different department.

Business rules:
- Employee must be ACTIVE.
- Target department must have headcount capacity.
- Salary adjustment must stay within the new position's grade band.
- Transfer cannot happen during an active leave.
functions.summary: Orchestrates the transfer of an employee to a new department by validating active status, checking for concurrent leave, verifying target department headcount capacity, validating any salary adjustment against the new grade band, updating the employee record, and recording the transfer history.

functions.name: EmployeeService.promote_employee
functions.docstring:
Promote an employee to a higher position.

Business rules:
- Employee must be ACTIVE and have at least 1 year of tenure.
- New salary must be higher than current salary.
- New salary must fall within the target position's grade band.
- Salary increase cannot exceed 30% without VP approval flag.
functions.summary: Orchestrates an employee promotion by verifying eligibility (tenure and status), enforcing salary increase constraints including a 30% cap requiring VP approval, validating the new salary against the target position's grade band, updating the employee record, and recording the promotion history.

functions.name: EmployeeService.terminate_employee
functions.docstring:
Terminate an employee and initiate offboarding.

Business rules:
- Cannot terminate an already-terminated employee.
- Termination triggers: benefit COBRA eligibility, final paycheck,
  access revocation, and equipment return.
- Involuntary terminations require a documented reason.
- PTO balance is paid out on termination (state law dependent).
functions.summary: Orchestrates the full employee termination lifecycle, enforcing termination rules and triggering all downstream offboarding workflows including final paycheck, COBRA eligibility, access revocation, and leave cancellation.

functions.name: EmployeeService.get_direct_reports
functions.docstring: Return all employees who report directly to the given manager.
functions.summary: Retrieves the list of employees who directly report to a specified manager from the data store.

functions.name: EmployeeService.get_org_tree
functions.docstring:
Build a hierarchical org tree starting from a given employee.

Returns a nested dict: {employee, direct_reports: [{employee, direct_reports: …}]}
functions.summary: Constructs a hierarchical organisational chart tree starting from a given employee, representing the reporting structure up to a configurable depth.

functions.name: EmployeeService._build_org_node
functions.summary: Recursively assembles a nested dictionary node representing an employee and their direct reports for use in org tree construction.

functions.name: EmployeeService._get_employee_or_raise
functions.summary: Fetches an employee record by ID from the data store and raises a standardised error if the employee does not exist.

functions.name: EmployeeService._trigger_onboarding
functions.docstring: Kick off onboarding tasks: IT setup, badge, orientation, etc.
functions.summary: Initiates the employee onboarding workflow by creating onboarding tasks such as IT setup, badge provisioning, and orientation scheduling.

functions.name: EmployeeService._cancel_pending_leaves
functions.docstring: Cancel any pending or approved future leave requests.
functions.summary: Cancels all pending or approved future leave requests for an employee being terminated, ensuring leave records are updated consistently during offboarding.

functions.name: EmployeeService._trigger_final_paycheck
functions.docstring: Schedule the final paycheck including PTO payout.
functions.summary: Schedules the final paycheck for a terminated employee, including any owed PTO payout, as part of the offboarding workflow.

functions.name: EmployeeService._trigger_cobra_eligibility
functions.docstring: Notify benefits team to send COBRA continuation notice.
functions.summary: Applies the business rule that only full-time employees are eligible for COBRA continuation coverage upon termination, and notifies the benefits team accordingly.

functions.name: EmployeeService._revoke_system_access
functions.docstring: Disable all system accounts and revoke badge access.
functions.summary: Revokes all system accounts and badge access for a terminated employee as part of the offboarding security process.

functions.name: LeaveService.submit_leave_request
functions.docstring:
Submit a new leave request.

Business rules:
- Employee must be ACTIVE or in PROBATION.
- Start date must be in the future (or today).
- End date must be on or after start date.
- Employee must have sufficient leave balance (for PTO/sick).
- Cannot have overlapping approved leave.
- Probationary employees cannot take PTO (only sick leave).
- FMLA requires 12 months of employment and 1250 hours worked.
functions.summary: Orchestrates the end-to-end submission of a new employee leave request, enforcing eligibility rules, checking balances, preventing overlaps, and persisting the request.

functions.name: LeaveService.approve_leave
functions.docstring:
Approve a pending leave request.

Business rules:
- Only pending requests can be approved.
- Approver must be the employee's manager or an HR admin.
- Approver cannot approve their own leave.
functions.summary: Processes the approval of a pending leave request by verifying approver authority and updating the request status.

functions.name: LeaveService.deny_leave
functions.docstring:
Deny a pending leave request and release reserved balance.

Business rules:
- A denial reason is required.
- Reserved balance days must be released back.
functions.summary: Processes the denial of a pending leave request, records the denial reason, and releases any previously reserved leave balance back to the employee.

functions.name: LeaveService.cancel_leave
functions.docstring:
Cancel a pending or approved leave request.

Business rules:
- Only the requesting employee can cancel.
- Cannot cancel leave that is already in progress or completed.
- Approved leave cancelled less than 24 hours before start requires manager notification.
functions.summary: Handles the cancellation of a pending or approved leave request, releases reserved balance, and triggers a manager notification for late cancellations.

functions.name: LeaveService.get_leave_balance
functions.docstring: Get an employee's leave balance for a given type and year.
functions.summary: Retrieves (or initialises) an employee's leave balance for a specified leave type and year, defaulting to the current year.

functions.name: LeaveService.calculate_pto_accrual
functions.docstring:
Calculate annual PTO accrual based on tenure.

Accrual tiers:
- 0-2 years: 15 days/year
- 2-5 years: 20 days/year
- 5-10 years: 25 days/year
- 10+ years: 30 days/year
functions.summary: Determines the annual PTO accrual entitlement for an employee based on their tenure, applying tiered accrual rates (15/20/25/30 days per year).

functions.name: LeaveService.run_year_end_carryover
functions.docstring:
Process year-end PTO carryover for all employees.

Business rules:
- Maximum of 5 PTO days can carry over to the next year.
- Excess PTO is forfeited ("use it or lose it").
- Sick leave does not carry over.
- Carryover is calculated from available (unused, non-pending) balance.
functions.summary: Orchestrates the annual year-end PTO carryover cycle for all active employees, applying carryover caps, forfeiting excess balances, and initialising next-year balances with new accruals.

functions.name: LeaveService._validate_fmla_eligibility
functions.docstring:
Validate FMLA eligibility per federal requirements.

Requirements:
- Employed for at least 12 months.
- Worked at least 1,250 hours in the past 12 months.
- Employer has 50+ employees within 75 miles.
functions.summary: Validates whether an employee meets the federally mandated FMLA eligibility criteria: minimum 12 months of employment and 1,250 hours worked in the past 12 months.

functions.name: LeaveService._get_or_create_balance
functions.summary: Retrieves an existing leave balance for an employee or, if none exists, initialises and persists a new one with the correct entitlement based on leave type and accrual rules.

functions.name: LeaveService._notify_manager_late_cancellation
functions.docstring: Send notification when approved leave is cancelled with short notice.
functions.summary: Logs a warning notification to the system when an employee cancels previously approved leave with short notice, alerting the manager via the logging infrastructure.

functions.name: PayrollService.create_payroll_run
functions.docstring:
Create a new payroll run in draft status.

Business rules:
- Pay date must be on or after the period end date.
- Cannot create duplicate runs for the same period.
functions.summary: Creates a new payroll run in draft status, enforcing that pay date must be on or after period end date and that no duplicate runs exist for the same period.

functions.name: PayrollService.process_payroll
functions.docstring:
Process a payroll run: calculate pay for all eligible employees.

Business rules:
- Only draft runs can be processed.
- Only ACTIVE and ON_LEAVE employees are included.
- Terminated employees are excluded unless termination is within the period.
- Overtime is calculated for non-exempt employees.
functions.summary: Orchestrates the end-to-end processing of a payroll run by iterating over all eligible employees, calculating pay stubs, aggregating totals, and transitioning the run to 'processed' status.

functions.name: PayrollService.approve_payroll
functions.docstring:
Approve a processed payroll run for payment.

Business rules:
- Only processed runs can be approved.
- Approver must be a payroll admin or finance director.
- Approval triggers ACH payment initiation.
functions.summary: Approves a processed payroll run after validating approver authority, transitions the run to 'approved' status, and triggers ACH payment initiation.

functions.name: PayrollService._calculate_pay_stub
functions.docstring: Calculate a single employee's pay for the period.
functions.summary: Calculates a complete pay stub for a single employee for the pay period, applying gross pay, overtime, bonuses, commissions, tax withholding, and deductions according to compensation rules.

functions.name: PayrollService._calculate_gross_pay
functions.docstring:
Calculate gross pay for one pay period from annual salary.

Business rules:
- Full-time: annual salary / periods per year.
- Part-time: prorated based on scheduled hours.
- Unpaid leave days are deducted.
functions.summary: Calculates an employee's gross pay for a single pay period from their annual salary, applying proration rules for part-time employees and accounting for unpaid leave.

functions.name: PayrollService._calculate_overtime_pay
functions.docstring:
Calculate overtime pay at 1.5x the regular hourly rate.

FLSA requires non-exempt employees to receive overtime for hours
worked beyond 40 in a workweek.
functions.summary: Calculates overtime pay at 1.5x the regular hourly rate for hours worked beyond the standard threshold, implementing FLSA compliance requirements.

functions.name: PayrollService._calculate_tax_withholding
functions.docstring:
Calculate federal and state tax withholding for a pay period.

Implements:
- Progressive federal income tax brackets.
- Social Security tax (6.2%) up to wage base.
- Medicare tax (1.45%) + additional Medicare (0.9%) above threshold.
- State income tax (simplified flat rate by state).
functions.summary: Calculates all tax withholding obligations for a pay period, including federal income tax, Social Security, Medicare, additional Medicare for high earners, and state income tax.

functions.name: PayrollService._apply_federal_brackets
functions.docstring: Apply progressive federal tax brackets to annual income.
functions.summary: Applies the US federal progressive income tax bracket structure to an annual income figure to compute the total federal income tax owed.

functions.name: PayrollService._get_state_tax_rate
functions.docstring: Return the state income tax rate based on employee's work state.
functions.summary: Returns the applicable state income tax rate for an employee based on their work state, using a simplified flat-rate table per state.

functions.name: PayrollService._calculate_deductions
functions.docstring: Calculate benefit deductions for the pay period.
functions.summary: Calculates per-period benefit deductions for an employee by retrieving active benefit enrollments and converting monthly contribution amounts to the appropriate pay frequency.

functions.name: PayrollService._calculate_employer_taxes
functions.docstring: Calculate employer-side payroll taxes (FICA + FUTA).
functions.summary: Calculates the employer's share of payroll taxes including Social Security (FICA), Medicare, and FUTA, applying statutory wage bases and rates.

functions.name: PayrollService._get_eligible_employees
functions.docstring: Return employees eligible for this payroll run.
functions.summary: Determines which employees qualify for inclusion in a given payroll run, including active, on-leave, probationary, and recently terminated employees who are owed a final paycheck.

functions.name: PayrollService._periods_per_year
functions.summary: Maps a pay frequency enumeration to the corresponding number of pay periods per year, used to annualise or de-annualise salary figures.

functions.name: PayrollService._initiate_ach_payments
functions.docstring: Initiate ACH direct deposit payments for all stubs in the run.
functions.summary: Initiates ACH direct deposit payments for all pay stubs in an approved payroll run by retrieving employee bank details and triggering payment disbursement.

functions.name: ReportingService.headcount_report
functions.docstring:
Generate a headcount report broken down by department and status.

Includes active, on-leave, and probationary employees.
Excludes terminated employees.
functions.summary: Generates a structured headcount report as of a given date, aggregating employee counts by department, employment type, and status while excluding terminated employees.

functions.name: ReportingService.turnover_report
functions.docstring:
Calculate employee turnover rate for a given period.

Turnover rate = (Separations / Average Headcount) × 100

Business rules:
- Voluntary and involuntary separations are tracked separately.
- Turnover is broken down by department for targeted retention efforts.
- Industry benchmark comparison is included when available.
functions.summary: Calculates employee turnover rate for a given period, breaking down separations into voluntary and involuntary categories, analysing by department and tenure bucket, and computing overall, voluntary, and involuntary turnover rates against average headcount.

functions.name: ReportingService.compensation_report
functions.docstring:
Generate a compensation analysis report.

Includes salary distribution, compa-ratio analysis, and pay equity metrics.

Business rules:
- Compa-ratio = actual salary / midpoint of grade band.
- Compa-ratio below 0.80 flags underpayment risk.
- Compa-ratio above 1.20 flags overpayment or compression.
- Gender pay gap is calculated per department.
functions.domain: Human Capital Management / Compensation & Pay Equity
functions.summary: Generates a compensation analysis report by computing compa-ratios for each active employee against their grade band midpoint, flagging underpayment (compa-ratio < 0.80) and overpayment (compa-ratio > 1.20), and producing department-level salary statistics including average, min, and max salary.

functions.name: ReportingService.workforce_planning_metrics
functions.docstring:
Calculate workforce planning metrics for strategic HR decisions.

Metrics:
- Time to fill open positions (average days).
- Retirement eligibility (employees within 5 years of 65).
- Span of control (average direct reports per manager).
- Internal mobility rate (transfers + promotions / headcount).
functions.summary: Computes strategic workforce planning metrics including retirement eligibility risk (employees aged 60+), average span of control per manager, and average employee tenure, to support HR strategic decision-making.

business_rules.title: New Hire Enrollment Window (30-Day Grace Period)
business_rules.given_clause: An employee has been hired and their hire date is recorded in the system
business_rules.when_clause: The system checks whether the employee is within a valid enrollment window
business_rules.then_clause: The employee is considered eligible to enroll if the number of days elapsed since their hire date is less than or equal to 30 days
business_rules.business_impact: If removed, newly hired employees would lose their guaranteed onboarding enrollment opportunity and could be locked out of benefits unless open enrollment or a qualifying life event applies. This would violate standard HR policy and potentially labor regulations requiring timely benefits access for new hires.

business_rules.title: Open Enrollment Month Restriction (November Only)
business_rules.given_clause: An employee exists in the system regardless of hire date or life events
business_rules.when_clause: The system checks whether the employee is within a valid enrollment window
business_rules.then_clause: The employee is considered eligible to enroll if the current calendar month is November (open enrollment month)
business_rules.business_impact: If removed, the annual open enrollment period would be eliminated, preventing all employees from making benefit changes or enrollments during the designated company-wide enrollment cycle. This would disrupt workforce benefits planning and potentially violate contractual or regulatory obligations to provide annual enrollment opportunities.

business_rules.title: Qualifying Life Event Enrollment Eligibility
business_rules.given_clause: An employee has experienced a qualifying life event (e.g., marriage, birth of a child, divorce, adoption) that has been recorded in the system
business_rules.when_clause: The system checks whether the employee is within a valid enrollment window
business_rules.then_clause: The employee is considered eligible to enroll if a qualifying life event is associated with their employee record in the database
business_rules.business_impact: If removed, employees who experience major life changes (marriage, childbirth, loss of other coverage, etc.) would be unable to update their benefits outside of open enrollment. This would likely violate IRS Section 125 regulations and ERISA requirements that mandate special enrollment rights following qualifying life events, exposing the organization to legal and compliance risk.

business_rules.title: Enrollment Window Default Denial
business_rules.given_clause: An employee does not meet any of the three valid enrollment window criteria: not within 30 days of hire, not in November, and has no qualifying life event
business_rules.when_clause: The system checks whether the employee is within a valid enrollment window
business_rules.then_clause: The employee is determined to be outside any valid enrollment window and is ineligible to enroll
business_rules.business_impact: If removed or inverted (i.e., defaulting to True), employees could enroll in benefits at any time throughout the year with no restrictions. This would undermine actuarial risk pooling, create adverse selection problems, increase administrative overhead, and potentially destabilize the organization's benefits cost structure.

business_rules.title: New Hire Coverage Effective Date: First of Month After 30 Days from Hire
business_rules.given_clause: An employee was hired within the new hire enrollment window (today minus hire date is within NEW_HIRE_ENROLLMENT_WINDOW_DAYS days), and their benefits effective date is being calculated
business_rules.when_clause: The effective date calculation is triggered during enrollment
business_rules.then_clause: The coverage effective date is set to the first day of the month immediately following the date that is 30 days after the employee's hire date
business_rules.business_impact: New hires would receive incorrect coverage start dates, potentially resulting in coverage gaps or premature coverage, creating compliance risk, incorrect premium billing, and potential legal liability if claims are denied due to wrong effective dates.

business_rules.title: New Hire 30-Day Waiting Period Before Coverage Begins
business_rules.given_clause: An employee is a new hire enrolled within the new hire enrollment window
business_rules.when_clause: The effective date is calculated
business_rules.then_clause: Coverage does not begin immediately on the hire date but is deferred by at least 30 days (to the first of the following month after 30 days), enforcing a mandatory waiting period
business_rules.business_impact: Removing the 30-day waiting period could allow new hires to receive immediate coverage, increasing insurance costs and potentially violating plan documents or ACA waiting period rules (which allow up to 90-day waiting periods). Insurers may reject claims or retroactively adjust premiums.

business_rules.title: Open Enrollment Coverage Effective Date: January 1 of Next Year
business_rules.given_clause: The current month is the designated open enrollment month (OPEN_ENROLLMENT_MONTH) and the employee is not a new hire within the enrollment window
business_rules.when_clause: The effective date calculation is triggered during enrollment
business_rules.then_clause: The coverage effective date is set to January 1 of the following calendar year
business_rules.business_impact: If removed, open enrollment elections could take effect immediately or on an incorrect date, disrupting plan year alignment, causing mid-year plan changes that violate IRS Section 125 cafeteria plan rules, and creating billing and reconciliation errors with insurance carriers.

business_rules.title: Life Event Coverage Effective Date: First of Next Month
business_rules.given_clause: The employee is not a new hire within the enrollment window and the current month is not the open enrollment month (i.e., the enrollment reason is a qualifying life event)
business_rules.when_clause: The effective date calculation is triggered during enrollment
business_rules.then_clause: The coverage effective date is set to the first day of the month immediately following the current month
business_rules.business_impact: If removed or altered, employees experiencing qualifying life events (marriage, birth, divorce, etc.) could receive incorrect coverage start dates, potentially leaving them uninsured during a critical period or triggering coverage before the insurer expects it, leading to claim denials or premium disputes.

business_rules.title: December Year-Boundary Handling for New Hire 30-Day Rule
business_rules.given_clause: A new hire's hire date plus 30 days falls in December (target.month == 12)
business_rules.when_clause: The effective date is calculated for a new hire
business_rules.then_clause: The coverage effective date correctly rolls over to January 1 of the next year rather than attempting to create an invalid month 13
business_rules.business_impact: Without this boundary check, the system would throw a runtime error (invalid month value of 13) for December new hires, causing enrollment failures and leaving employees without benefits coverage. Note: there is also a potential bug — when month rolls to January, the year is not incremented, which could produce an incorrect date in the prior year.

business_rules.title: December Year-Boundary Handling for Life Event Rule
business_rules.given_clause: A qualifying life event occurs in December (today.month == 12) and the employee is not a new hire within the window and it is not open enrollment month
business_rules.when_clause: The effective date is calculated for a life event
business_rules.then_clause: The coverage effective date correctly rolls over to January 1 of the next calendar year
business_rules.business_impact: Without this boundary check, a December life event would cause a runtime error or incorrect date, preventing enrollment processing for employees with qualifying life events in December and leaving them without updated coverage.

business_rules.title: Skip COBRA Enrollment for Unresolvable Benefit Plans
business_rules.given_clause: A terminated employee has an existing benefit enrollment referencing a plan ID
business_rules.when_clause: COBRA initiation is triggered and the plan record cannot be retrieved from the database
business_rules.then_clause: The COBRA enrollment for that specific plan is silently skipped and processing continues for remaining enrollments
business_rules.business_impact: Without this guard, COBRA enrollments could be created for invalid or deleted plans, resulting in employees being billed for non-existent coverage and creating orphaned enrollment records with no valid plan backing. Removing it could also cause runtime errors that halt COBRA processing for all remaining valid plans.

business_rules.title: COBRA Premium Equals Full Employee Plus Employer Contribution
business_rules.given_clause: A terminated employee is eligible for COBRA continuation coverage on an active enrollment
business_rules.when_clause: A COBRA enrollment record is created
business_rules.then_clause: The monthly COBRA cost is calculated as the sum of the original employee contribution and the original employer contribution (i.e., the full group premium), before any administrative fee is applied
business_rules.business_impact: If removed or altered (e.g., using only the employee contribution), COBRA participants would be undercharged, causing the organization to subsidize continuation coverage it is legally not required to fund. This violates COBRA regulations (ERISA/IRS rules) requiring the full group rate to be charged to the former employee.

business_rules.title: COBRA Premium Includes 2% Administrative Fee
business_rules.given_clause: The full group premium (employee + employer share) has been calculated for a COBRA enrollee
business_rules.when_clause: The COBRA monthly cost is finalized
business_rules.then_clause: An additional 2% administrative fee is applied on top of the full premium cost (multiplied by 1.02)
business_rules.business_impact: Removing the 2% fee means the organization loses the legally permitted administrative cost recovery allowed under COBRA regulations (up to 2% per 26 U.S.C. § 4980B). Inverting it (e.g., applying a discount) would result in the organization subsidizing administration costs, creating financial losses at scale.

business_rules.title: Employer Bears No Cost Under COBRA
business_rules.given_clause: A COBRA enrollment record is being created for a terminated employee
business_rules.when_clause: The enrollment is persisted
business_rules.then_clause: The employer contribution on the COBRA enrollment is set to zero, placing the entire premium burden (including the administrative fee) on the former employee
business_rules.business_impact: If the employer contribution were set to a non-zero value, the organization would be financially subsidizing COBRA coverage it is not obligated to fund, creating unbudgeted benefit costs and potentially creating inconsistent treatment across terminated employees. It would also misrepresent the cost-sharing structure in enrollment records.

business_rules.title: COBRA Coverage Begins the Day After Benefit End Date
business_rules.given_clause: A terminated employee's active benefit coverage has an end date
business_rules.when_clause: A COBRA enrollment record is created
business_rules.then_clause: The COBRA effective date is set to exactly one calendar day after the benefit end date, ensuring no gap in coverage eligibility
business_rules.business_impact: If the effective date were set to the same day as benefit_end, there would be a one-day overlap creating potential double-coverage liability. If set to two or more days later, a coverage gap would exist, exposing the employee to uninsured medical costs and potentially creating legal liability for the organization under COBRA notice and coverage continuity requirements.

business_rules.title: COBRA Enrollment Preserves Original Coverage Level and Dependents
business_rules.given_clause: A terminated employee had an active enrollment with a specific coverage level and dependent count
business_rules.when_clause: A COBRA enrollment record is created
business_rules.then_clause: The COBRA enrollment inherits the same coverage level and dependent count as the original active enrollment
business_rules.business_impact: If coverage level or dependent count were not preserved, COBRA enrollees could lose dependent coverage or have their coverage tier changed without consent, violating COBRA regulations that require continuation of the same coverage that was in effect at the time of the qualifying event. This could expose the organization to legal claims and regulatory penalties.

business_rules.title: COBRA Enrollment Status Is Distinctly Classified
business_rules.given_clause: A new enrollment record is being created for a terminated employee under COBRA
business_rules.when_clause: The enrollment is persisted to the database
business_rules.then_clause: The enrollment status is set to EnrollmentStatus.COBRA, distinguishing it from active, terminated, or other enrollment states
business_rules.business_impact: Without a distinct COBRA status, COBRA enrollments would be indistinguishable from active employer-sponsored enrollments in reporting, billing, and compliance systems. This would corrupt benefits reporting, potentially cause incorrect premium billing, and make it impossible to track COBRA-specific obligations such as the 18-month maximum coverage period.

business_rules.title: COBRA Enrollment ID Uses Prefixed Unique Identifier
business_rules.given_clause: A COBRA enrollment record is being created
business_rules.when_clause: The enrollment ID is assigned
business_rules.then_clause: The enrollment ID is prefixed with 'COBRA-' followed by an 8-character uppercase hexadecimal UUID fragment, ensuring COBRA records are uniquely and visibly identifiable
business_rules.business_impact: Without the 'COBRA-' prefix, COBRA enrollments cannot be easily distinguished from standard enrollments by ID alone, complicating audits, customer service lookups, and compliance reporting. Using only 8 hex characters (from a UUID) introduces a small but non-zero collision risk (~1 in 4 billion) which could cause data integrity issues if enrollment IDs must be unique.

business_rules.title: Employee Must Be Active and Have Minimum One Year Tenure for Promotion
business_rules.given_clause: An employee exists in the system with a given employee ID
business_rules.when_clause: A promotion is requested for that employee
business_rules.then_clause: The promotion is rejected with an error if the employee is not in ACTIVE status or has less than 1 year of tenure
business_rules.business_impact: Without this rule, terminated, suspended, or newly hired employees could receive promotions, creating legal exposure, payroll inconsistencies, and undermining the integrity of the career development process. New hires could be promoted before demonstrating any performance, and inactive employees could receive salary increases they are not entitled to.

business_rules.title: Promotion Salary Must Exceed Current Salary
business_rules.given_clause: An eligible employee is being considered for promotion
business_rules.when_clause: A new salary is proposed that is equal to or lower than the employee's current base salary
business_rules.then_clause: The promotion is rejected with an error indicating the new salary must be higher than the current salary
business_rules.business_impact: Without this rule, a promotion could be recorded with no salary improvement or even a pay cut, violating the fundamental definition of a promotion as career advancement. This could lead to employee grievances, legal disputes over compensation, and corruption of compensation analytics and benchmarking data.

business_rules.title: Salary Increase Exceeding 30% Requires VP Approval
business_rules.given_clause: An eligible employee is being promoted with a salary increase greater than 30% of their current base salary
business_rules.when_clause: The promotion is processed
business_rules.then_clause: A warning is logged indicating that VP approval is required for the salary increase, flagging the transaction for oversight
business_rules.business_impact: Without this rule, managers could grant excessive salary increases without executive oversight, leading to budget overruns, pay equity violations, and potential abuse of the promotion process. Note: the current implementation only logs a warning rather than blocking the transaction, meaning VP approval is not actually enforced programmatically — removal would eliminate even the audit trail of such events.

business_rules.title: New Salary Must Fall Within the Target Position's Grade Band
business_rules.given_clause: An eligible employee is being promoted to a new job title that has a defined salary grade band in the system
business_rules.when_clause: The proposed new salary falls below the minimum or above the maximum of the target position's grade band
business_rules.then_clause: The promotion is rejected with an error indicating the salary is outside the grade band for the target position
business_rules.business_impact: Without this rule, employees could be promoted with salaries that violate the organisation's compensation structure, causing pay inequity among peers in the same role, budget control failures, and non-compliance with internal compensation policies or external regulatory requirements. It would also undermine the integrity of the grade band system used for workforce planning and benchmarking.

business_rules.title: Promotion History Must Be Recorded Upon Successful Promotion
business_rules.given_clause: An employee promotion has passed all eligibility and salary validations
business_rules.when_clause: The employee record is updated with the new job title and salary
business_rules.then_clause: A promotion history record is persisted capturing the old title, new title, old salary, and new salary for audit and reporting purposes
business_rules.business_impact: Without this rule, there would be no audit trail of promotion events, making it impossible to reconstruct an employee's career history, investigate compensation disputes, comply with labour law record-keeping requirements, or produce accurate HR analytics on promotion rates and salary progression.

business_rules.title: Mandatory Audit Trail for Sensitive HR Actions
business_rules.given_clause: A sensitive HR action (e.g., hire, terminate, salary change) has been initiated by an actor on a specific entity
business_rules.when_clause: Any sensitive HR action is performed within the system
business_rules.then_clause: A structured audit event must be recorded capturing the event type, entity type, entity ID, actor ID, action details, UTC timestamp, and originating IP address, and persisted to the compliance audit store
business_rules.business_impact: Removal would eliminate the compliance audit trail entirely, exposing the organisation to regulatory violations (e.g., GDPR, SOX, HIPAA), failed audits, inability to investigate unauthorised or erroneous HR actions, and potential legal liability.

business_rules.title: Actor Identity Must Be Captured on Every Audit Event
business_rules.given_clause: An HR action is being logged for compliance purposes
business_rules.when_clause: An audit event is recorded
business_rules.then_clause: The identity of the actor who performed the action (actor_id) must be included in the audit record, establishing accountability and non-repudiation
business_rules.business_impact: Without actor identity, it becomes impossible to attribute actions to individuals, undermining accountability, non-repudiation, and the ability to investigate insider threats or unauthorised changes to employee records.

business_rules.title: UTC Timestamp Must Be Recorded at the Moment of the Audit Event
business_rules.given_clause: An HR audit event is being constructed for persistence
business_rules.when_clause: The audit event record is assembled
business_rules.then_clause: The current UTC date and time in ISO 8601 format must be captured at the instant of logging to provide an authoritative, timezone-neutral record of when the action occurred
business_rules.business_impact: Without a reliable UTC timestamp, audit records lose temporal integrity, making it impossible to establish accurate timelines for regulatory investigations, forensic analysis, or dispute resolution across time zones.

business_rules.title: Originating IP Address Must Be Captured for Every Audit Event
business_rules.given_clause: An HR audit event is being constructed for compliance tracking
business_rules.when_clause: The audit event record is assembled
business_rules.then_clause: The IP address from which the action originated must be retrieved and included in the audit record to support forensic traceability and detection of unauthorised access
business_rules.business_impact: Omitting the originating IP address removes a key forensic data point, hindering the ability to detect and investigate unauthorised remote access, credential misuse, or geographic anomalies in HR data modifications.

business_rules.title: Audit Event Must Identify the Target Entity
business_rules.given_clause: A sensitive HR action is performed on a specific HR entity (e.g., an employee record, a contract)
business_rules.when_clause: The audit event is recorded
business_rules.then_clause: Both the type of entity (entity_type) and its unique identifier (entity_id) must be captured in the audit record so that the exact subject of the action can be unambiguously identified
business_rules.business_impact: Without entity identification, audit records cannot be linked to specific HR objects, making it impossible to reconstruct the history of changes to a particular employee or contract, and rendering compliance reports meaningless.

business_rules.title: Audit Event Must Classify the Type of Action Performed
business_rules.given_clause: A sensitive HR action has been triggered
business_rules.when_clause: The audit event is recorded
business_rules.then_clause: The event type (e.g., hire, terminate, salary_change) must be explicitly recorded to categorise the nature of the action for compliance reporting and policy enforcement
business_rules.business_impact: Without event type classification, compliance teams cannot filter or report on specific categories of sensitive actions, making it impossible to demonstrate adherence to policies governing particular HR operations.

business_rules.title: Audit Event Must Include Contextual Details of the Action
business_rules.given_clause: A sensitive HR action is being logged
business_rules.when_clause: The audit event is recorded
business_rules.then_clause: A structured details payload capturing the contextual information (e.g., reason, changed values, approvals) of the action must be persisted alongside the core audit fields to satisfy the 'why' requirement of compliance logging
business_rules.business_impact: Without contextual details, audit records provide only a skeletal record of what happened but not why or how, severely limiting the usefulness of the audit trail for regulatory investigations, grievance procedures, or change management reviews.

business_rules.title: Audit Event Must Be Persisted to a Durable Compliance Store
business_rules.given_clause: A fully constructed audit event record exists in memory
business_rules.when_clause: The audit event has been assembled with all required compliance fields
business_rules.then_clause: The event must be durably persisted to the designated compliance audit store (DynamoDB audit table) to ensure it survives system restarts and is available for future regulatory review
business_rules.business_impact: Failure to persist the audit event means the compliance record exists only transiently in memory and will be lost, creating gaps in the audit trail that could constitute a regulatory breach and prevent forensic reconstruction of HR actions.

business_rules.title: Full-Time Employment Type Automatically Qualifies as ACA Full-Time
business_rules.given_clause: An employee exists in the system with an employment type explicitly set to FULL_TIME
business_rules.when_clause: An ACA full-time status determination is performed for that employee
business_rules.then_clause: The employee is immediately classified as ACA full-time without requiring an hours calculation
business_rules.business_impact: If removed, full-time salaried or classified employees who may not have tracked hours would be incorrectly excluded from ACA full-time status, potentially causing the employer to miss offering them required health coverage, resulting in ACA penalty exposure (employer shared responsibility payments under IRC §4980H) and possible regulatory violations.

business_rules.title: Average Weekly Hours Threshold of 30 Hours Determines ACA Full-Time Status
business_rules.given_clause: An employee exists whose employment type is NOT classified as FULL_TIME (e.g., part-time, variable-hour, or seasonal)
business_rules.when_clause: An ACA full-time status determination is performed and the employee's average weekly hours are retrieved from the database
business_rules.then_clause: The employee is classified as ACA full-time if and only if their average weekly hours are greater than or equal to 30 hours per week (ACA_FULL_TIME_HOURS_WEEKLY)
business_rules.business_impact: If removed or the threshold is changed, variable-hour or part-time employees averaging 30+ hours would be misclassified as non-full-time, causing the employer to unlawfully deny them health coverage offers. This directly violates ACA employer mandate rules (IRC §4980H(a)/(b)), exposing the organisation to significant IRS penalties (currently $2,970–$4,460 per affected employee per year) and potential employee lawsuits for benefits denial.

business_rules.title: Exclude Terminated Employees from I-9 Audit
business_rules.given_clause: An employee exists in the system with a status of TERMINATED
business_rules.when_clause: The I-9 compliance audit is executed
business_rules.then_clause: The terminated employee is skipped and not included in the compliance check or issue reporting
business_rules.business_impact: Terminated employees would be incorrectly flagged for missing or expired I-9 forms, generating false compliance violations, inflating issue counts, and wasting HR resources investigating non-issues. It could also create legal confusion about obligations for former employees.

business_rules.title: Missing I-9 Form is Critical After Section 2 Deadline
business_rules.given_clause: An active employee has no I-9 record on file and has been employed for more than the Section 2 deadline number of business days
business_rules.when_clause: The I-9 compliance audit is executed
business_rules.then_clause: A critical-severity issue is raised for that employee, reporting the form as missing and calculating the number of days overdue
business_rules.business_impact: Employers would not be alerted to missing I-9 forms past the legal deadline, exposing the organization to federal fines ranging from $252 to $2,507 per missing form. Regulatory audits (e.g., by ICE) could result in significant financial penalties and reputational damage.

business_rules.title: I-9 Section 1 Must Be Completed
business_rules.given_clause: An active employee has an I-9 record on file
business_rules.when_clause: The I-9 compliance audit is executed and Section 1 of the I-9 is not marked as complete
business_rules.then_clause: A high-severity issue is raised indicating that I-9 Section 1 is incomplete for that employee
business_rules.business_impact: Section 1 must legally be completed by the employee on or before their first day of employment. Failing to detect incomplete Section 1 forms means the employer is non-compliant with USCIS regulations, risking fines and potential liability for employing individuals without verified eligibility documentation.

business_rules.title: I-9 Section 2 Must Be Completed
business_rules.given_clause: An active employee has an I-9 record on file
business_rules.when_clause: The I-9 compliance audit is executed and Section 2 of the I-9 is not marked as complete
business_rules.then_clause: A high-severity issue is raised indicating that I-9 Section 2 is incomplete for that employee
business_rules.business_impact: Section 2 must legally be completed by the employer within 3 business days of the employee's start date. Missing this detection means employers may unknowingly retain incomplete I-9 forms, violating federal law and exposing the company to per-form fines during audits.

business_rules.title: Expired Work Authorization is a Critical Compliance Violation
business_rules.given_clause: An active employee has an I-9 record with a work authorization expiry date, and that date has already passed
business_rules.when_clause: The I-9 compliance audit is executed
business_rules.then_clause: A critical-severity issue is raised indicating the work authorization has expired, along with the number of days since expiration
business_rules.business_impact: Continuing to employ a worker with expired work authorization violates federal immigration law and can result in severe penalties including substantial fines and potential criminal liability for the employer. Removing this rule would allow the organization to unknowingly employ unauthorized workers.

business_rules.title: Work Authorization Expiring Within 90 Days Triggers a Warning
business_rules.given_clause: An active employee has an I-9 record with a work authorization expiry date that has not yet passed
business_rules.when_clause: The I-9 compliance audit is executed and the work authorization will expire within 90 days or fewer
business_rules.then_clause: A warning-severity issue is raised indicating the number of days until the work authorization expires, prompting proactive reverification
business_rules.business_impact: Without a 90-day advance warning, HR teams would have no lead time to initiate reverification before expiration. This would increase the likelihood of employees working with expired authorizations, creating critical violations and potential legal exposure that could have been avoided with timely action.

business_rules.title: Compliance Report Marks Organization as Non-Compliant When Any Issue Exists
business_rules.given_clause: The I-9 compliance audit has been executed across all active employees
business_rules.when_clause: One or more compliance issues of any severity are identified
business_rules.then_clause: The compliance report sets the 'compliant' flag to false, and the report includes a total issue count, a critical issue count, and a UTC timestamp of the audit
business_rules.business_impact: If the compliant flag were always set to true or not evaluated correctly, downstream systems or dashboards relying on this flag would incorrectly report the organization as fully compliant, masking real violations and preventing escalation or remediation workflows from triggering.

business_rules.title: Critical and Total Issue Counts Are Logged as Warnings When Issues Exist
business_rules.given_clause: The I-9 compliance audit has completed and at least one issue has been identified
business_rules.when_clause: The compliance result is being finalized
business_rules.then_clause: A warning-level log entry is emitted containing the total number of issues and the number of critical issues, ensuring operational visibility
business_rules.business_impact: Without this logging, compliance violations would be silently recorded in the database without any operational alerting. Monitoring systems and on-call teams would not receive signals about critical I-9 issues, delaying human review and increasing the window of regulatory exposure.

business_rules.title: EEO-1 Filing Obligation Threshold (100+ Employees)
business_rules.domain: HR Compliance / Equal Employment Opportunity Reporting
business_rules.given_clause: An employer is generating an EEO-1 report for a given report year and the total number of qualifying employees has been counted
business_rules.when_clause: The total employee count is evaluated against the federal filing threshold
business_rules.then_clause: The report must indicate 'filing_required' as true if and only if the total employee count is 100 or more; otherwise it is false
business_rules.business_impact: If removed or inverted, employers below the 100-employee threshold could be incorrectly flagged as obligated to file (causing unnecessary compliance burden), or employers at or above the threshold could be told filing is not required, exposing them to EEOC enforcement actions, fines, and legal liability for non-compliance with federal law (42 U.S.C. § 2000e-8).

business_rules.title: Workforce Demographics Reported by Job Category
business_rules.domain: HR Compliance / Equal Employment Opportunity Reporting
business_rules.given_clause: A set of employees is retrieved for the report year
business_rules.when_clause: The EEO-1 report matrix is constructed
business_rules.then_clause: Each employee must be classified into exactly one EEOC-defined EEO-1 job category, and their count must be recorded within that category in the report matrix
business_rules.business_impact: If removed, the report would not segment workforce data by job category, violating the EEO-1 Component 1 reporting format required by the EEOC. The submitted report would be invalid, potentially resulting in rejection, re-filing requirements, or regulatory penalties.

business_rules.title: Workforce Demographics Reported by Race/Ethnicity and Gender Combination
business_rules.domain: HR Compliance / Equal Employment Opportunity Reporting
business_rules.given_clause: An employee record is being processed for inclusion in the EEO-1 report matrix
business_rules.when_clause: A demographic key is constructed for the employee
business_rules.then_clause: The demographic key must combine the employee's race/ethnicity and gender as a composite identifier (format: '{race}_{gender}'), and the employee must be counted under that combined demographic within their job category
business_rules.business_impact: If removed, the report would lose the race/ethnicity and gender breakdown required by the EEOC EEO-1 Component 1 form. The resulting report would be non-compliant with federal reporting requirements, rendering it legally invalid and potentially triggering EEOC enforcement action.

business_rules.title: Default 'Not Specified' for Missing Race/Ethnicity Data
business_rules.domain: HR Compliance / Equal Employment Opportunity Reporting
business_rules.given_clause: An employee record is being processed and the 'race' field is absent or null
business_rules.when_clause: The demographic key is constructed for that employee
business_rules.then_clause: The race/ethnicity component of the demographic key must default to 'Not Specified' rather than raising an error or omitting the employee
business_rules.business_impact: If removed, employees with missing race/ethnicity data could be silently dropped from the report or cause processing errors, leading to an undercount of the workforce. This could misrepresent the employer's workforce composition to the EEOC and potentially mask data quality issues that regulators expect to be disclosed.

business_rules.title: Default 'Not Specified' for Missing Gender Data
business_rules.domain: HR Compliance / Equal Employment Opportunity Reporting
business_rules.given_clause: An employee record is being processed and the 'gender' field is absent or null
business_rules.when_clause: The demographic key is constructed for that employee
business_rules.then_clause: The gender component of the demographic key must default to 'Not Specified' rather than raising an error or omitting the employee
business_rules.business_impact: If removed, employees with missing gender data could be silently dropped from the report or cause processing errors, leading to an undercount of the workforce. This could misrepresent the employer's workforce composition to the EEOC and potentially mask data quality issues that regulators expect to be disclosed.

business_rules.title: EEO-1 Report Scoped to a Single Calendar/Report Year
business_rules.domain: HR Compliance / Equal Employment Opportunity Reporting
business_rules.given_clause: A request is made to generate an EEO-1 report
business_rules.when_clause: Employee data is retrieved and the report is assembled
business_rules.then_clause: All employee data, the report metadata, and the total employee count must be scoped exclusively to the specified report year, and the report must record that year as 'report_year'
business_rules.business_impact: If removed, the report could aggregate employees across multiple years, producing inflated or inaccurate headcounts and demographic distributions. This would result in a materially false EEO-1 filing, which carries legal risk under federal anti-discrimination and reporting statutes.

business_rules.title: Report Must Include Total Employee Count
business_rules.domain: HR Compliance / Equal Employment Opportunity Reporting
business_rules.given_clause: Employee data for the report year has been retrieved and aggregated
business_rules.when_clause: The EEO-1 report object is assembled
business_rules.then_clause: The report must include the total count of all employees considered for the reporting period as 'total_employees'
business_rules.business_impact: If removed, the filing obligation determination would have no basis, and downstream consumers of the report (e.g., HR systems, compliance officers) would lack the aggregate headcount needed to verify the accuracy of the filing or make the filing/no-filing decision.

business_rules.title: Report Generation Timestamp Must Be Recorded in UTC
business_rules.domain: HR Compliance / Equal Employment Opportunity Reporting
business_rules.given_clause: An EEO-1 report has been successfully generated
business_rules.when_clause: The report object is assembled
business_rules.then_clause: The report must include a 'generated_at' timestamp representing the exact UTC date and time the report was produced, in ISO 8601 format
business_rules.business_impact: If removed, there would be no audit trail of when the report was generated, making it difficult to demonstrate timely compliance, resolve disputes about filing deadlines, or support internal audit and legal discovery requirements.

business_rules.title: Executive/Senior Officials Classification
business_rules.domain: HR Compliance / Equal Employment Opportunity Reporting
business_rules.given_clause: An employee record exists with a job title containing executive-level keywords
business_rules.when_clause: The EEO-1 category mapping is performed for an employee whose job title (case-insensitive) contains any of: 'vp', 'director', 'chief', 'president', 'ceo', 'cfo', 'cto'
business_rules.then_clause: The employee is classified under the EEOC EEO-1 category 'Executive/Senior Officials'
business_rules.business_impact: Executive-level employees would be misclassified into lower EEO-1 categories (e.g., 'First/Mid-Level Officials' or 'Professionals'), causing inaccurate EEO-1 Component 1 filings, potential EEOC regulatory violations, and distorted workforce diversity reporting at the senior leadership level.

business_rules.title: First/Mid-Level Officials and Managers Classification
business_rules.domain: HR Compliance / Equal Employment Opportunity Reporting
business_rules.given_clause: An employee record exists with a job title containing managerial or supervisory keywords, and the title does not match any executive-level keyword
business_rules.when_clause: The EEO-1 category mapping is performed for an employee whose job title (case-insensitive) contains any of: 'manager', 'supervisor', 'lead', 'head'
business_rules.then_clause: The employee is classified under the EEOC EEO-1 category 'First/Mid-Level Officials'
business_rules.business_impact: Mid-level managers and supervisors would be misclassified as 'Professionals' or another category, skewing the management-layer demographics in EEO-1 reports and potentially masking disparities in managerial representation required for EEOC compliance analysis.

business_rules.title: Professionals Classification
business_rules.domain: HR Compliance / Equal Employment Opportunity Reporting
business_rules.given_clause: An employee record exists with a job title containing professional/technical knowledge-worker keywords, and the title does not match executive or managerial keywords
business_rules.when_clause: The EEO-1 category mapping is performed for an employee whose job title (case-insensitive) contains any of: 'engineer', 'developer', 'analyst', 'scientist', 'architect'
business_rules.then_clause: The employee is classified under the EEOC EEO-1 category 'Professionals'
business_rules.business_impact: Engineers, developers, analysts, and scientists would fall through to the default 'Professionals' category only if no other rule matched, but if the default were different, these roles would be misclassified. Removal of this explicit rule reduces clarity and maintainability, and any future default change would silently misclassify a large portion of the workforce.

business_rules.title: Technicians Classification
business_rules.domain: HR Compliance / Equal Employment Opportunity Reporting
business_rules.given_clause: An employee record exists with a job title containing technician or specialist keywords, and the title does not match executive, managerial, or professional keywords
business_rules.when_clause: The EEO-1 category mapping is performed for an employee whose job title (case-insensitive) contains any of: 'technician', 'specialist', 'support'
business_rules.then_clause: The employee is classified under the EEOC EEO-1 category 'Technicians'
business_rules.business_impact: Technicians and specialists would be misclassified as 'Professionals' (via the default), inflating the Professionals count and deflating the Technicians count in EEO-1 filings, leading to inaccurate occupational category demographics submitted to the EEOC.

business_rules.title: Sales Workers Classification
business_rules.domain: HR Compliance / Equal Employment Opportunity Reporting
business_rules.given_clause: An employee record exists with a job title containing sales-related keywords, and the title does not match executive, managerial, professional, or technician keywords
business_rules.when_clause: The EEO-1 category mapping is performed for an employee whose job title (case-insensitive) contains any of: 'sales', 'account'
business_rules.then_clause: The employee is classified under the EEOC EEO-1 category 'Sales Workers'
business_rules.business_impact: Sales personnel and account managers/executives would be misclassified as 'Professionals' by default, distorting the Sales Workers demographic cell in the EEO-1 matrix and potentially misrepresenting gender and race/ethnicity distribution in customer-facing roles.

business_rules.title: Administrative Support Classification
business_rules.domain: HR Compliance / Equal Employment Opportunity Reporting
